title: Representation (Fold)
description: A reader's structured read of a span — the fold — as distinct from the compression of that read against a basis (measurement.schema.json). Carried by observation.representation.computed (SPEC.md §4.4a). The shape here is deliberately close to what an EO reader (e.g. eoreader4.2's src/perceiver/reading.js) already produces per span: a set of operator-tagged events at a grain, plus the two surprisal channels (novelty and Bayesian) that measure how much the read diverged from the reader's own running prior. src/fold.js turns this into a phasepost_measurements-shaped compression (eo-fold-compression@1.0.0) WITHOUT re-deriving it from an embedding — this is the structural, 'actually read' alternative to src/compress.js's cosine-against-centroids path, not a wrapper around it.

operatorKey.description: One of the nine EO operators (data/phasepost-cells.json 'operators'). Must match a key there; that file, not this enum, is the source of truth if the two ever drift.

grainKey.description: Which of the three grains this operator event was read at. Figure: the specific committed thing at this span (most per-span reader events land here). Ground: an ambient/prior condition the span was read against, not asserted by it. Pattern: an already-recurring identity or relation the span merely re-instances — reserved for the projector's own Figure→Pattern condensation (src/emergence.js); a single reader pass over one span should not be claiming Pattern-grain evidence itself.

operatorEvent.description: One piece of operator-tagged evidence from the read — e.g. reading.js's per-span `surprises` entries (each already carries an `op`), or the held/steady case (no surprise fired: NUL at Figure grain, non-transformation). weight_ppm is this event's own strength within the span's read, NOT yet normalized against the other events or against the 27-cell space — src/fold.js does that aggregation.

weight_ppm.description: Parts-per-million scaled non-negative strength (SPEC.md §4.1, invariant 4: no floats). Not a probability by itself — see src/fold.js's aggregation.

fold.description: The reader's structured read of one span. Deliberately NOT 27 cells wide — that projection is measurement's job, not representation's; a fold reports what the read actually found, in the reader's own vocabulary, and may find zero, one, or several operator events.

reader_version.description: Which versioned reader produced this fold (e.g. eoreader4.2's perceiver version). A fold-basis and every fold measured against it are version-coupled to this exactly as lens.js's lens_id already couples a reading to its Lens — regenerating the pool with a different reader_version is a new basis, not an update to the old one.

lens_id.description: The reader's own Lens address for this read (eoreader4.2 perceiver/lens.js: '<horizon>@γ<gamma>[+<name>@<hash>]'). Two folds of the same span read under two different lenses are not comparable and must not be pooled into one basis without both lenses being declared (mirrors invariant 11).

operator_events.description: Zero or more operator-tagged evidence pieces. Empty is legal and meaningful: a span read with nothing surprising and no bond/definition/instantiation firing is itself evidence (folds to NUL at Figure grain by src/fold.js's own default, not asserted here).

surprisal_bits.description: The NOVELTY channel (−log₂p), scaled to micro-bits (bits * 1e6) to stay an integer (invariant 4) — reading.js's surprisalBits, the audit/trace channel: how improbable this span was under the reader's running prior.

bayes_bits.description: The SIGNIFICANCE channel (D_KL(posterior‖prior)), scaled to micro-bits — reading.js's bayesBits: how far the read moved the reader's belief, which can diagnostically disagree with surprisal_bits (Itti & Baldi's TV-snow case: maximally improbable, moves no belief).

held.description: True when the span confirmed the reader's prediction and fired no operator event — reading.js's `held`. The absence of a surprise is itself a read, not a null result; src/fold.js folds a held span with no operator_events to NUL_Dissecting_Entity (hold / non-transformation) rather than to an empty, unscoreable measurement.
